seo.imageAlt: Musclog app preview showing private fitness, nutrition, and progress tracking.
seo.keywords: Musclog, fitness tracker, workout tracker, nutrition tracker, macro tracker, open source fitness app, local-first app

home.title: Musclog - Lift, Log, Repeat
home.description: A free, open-source, local-first fitness and nutrition tracker with workout logging, AI meal tracking, progress analytics, and private on-device data.

download.title: Download Musclog
download.description: Download Musclog for Android or join the iOS TestFlight and start tracking workouts, nutrition, and progress with your data stored privately on your device.

exercises.title: Musclog Exercise Library
exercises.description: Browse Musclog's exercise library with muscle groups, equipment, mechanics, instructions, and GIF-style visuals for building better workouts.

gameboy.title: Play Musclog on Game Boy
gameboy.description: Play the tiny Game Boy Color version of Musclog in your browser and download the ROM for a pocket-sized fitness tracking throwback.

progress.title: Musclog Progress Import
progress.description: Import a Musclog JSON export locally in your browser to explore weight, nutrition, workout, mood, and measurement trends on a desktop dashboard.

repMarker.title: Rep Marker Tool | Musclog
repMarker.description: Upload rep recordings, mark intervals, preview motion charts, and export labeled training data for Musclog's rep-counting tools.

calculator.title: Fitness and Macro Calculator | Musclog
calculator.description: Estimate calories, macros, BMI, FFMI, and goal targets with Musclog's fitness calculator, then use the plan in the app.

faq.title: Musclog FAQ
faq.description: Find answers about Musclog's privacy model, nutrition math, workout tracking, AI features, and open-source fitness methodology.

contact.title: Contact Musclog
contact.description: Contact Musclog for support, questions, bug reports, or open-source collaboration around private fitness and nutrition tracking.

privacy.title: Musclog Privacy Policy
privacy.description: Read how Musclog handles app data, website analytics, optional AI providers, local-first storage, encryption, permissions, and your privacy choices.

terms.title: Musclog Terms and Conditions
terms.description: Review the terms and conditions for using Musclog, including service scope, liability, warranties, governing law, and contact details.

test.title: Debug Chart Uploader | Musclog
test.description: A noindex development route for uploading and previewing rep chart data while building Musclog's recording tools.

landing.title: Musclog Web Demo
landing.tagline: AI-powered fitness & nutrition tracking — free & open source.

hero.subheading: A local-first, open-source fitness tracker. No accounts, no tracking, and no cloud. Just you and your gains.
hero.demoDescription: Open a browser-based preview of the app and explore the experience before downloading.

carousel.title: A quick look inside Musclog
carousel.description: Swipe through a few real screens from the app to see the nutrition, workout, and progress flows in action.

nutrition.title: Nutrition dashboard

home.description: See your day, recent foods, and training shortcuts in one place.

mealLogging.title: Meal logging
mealLogging.description: Log meals and servings with a nutrition-first workflow.

progress.title: Progress insights
progress.description: Review body metrics and trends that make change easier to spot.

workouts.title: Workout library
workouts.description: Browse and launch your training plans from one place.

screenshots.title: See Musclog in motion
screenshots.description: A slow scrolling preview of real app screens. Hover to pause and click any card to open it larger.

home.title: Home overview
home.description: Daily dashboard, recent foods, and quick training shortcuts in one place.

completeSet.title: Complete set
completeSet.description: Finish the set and move straight to the next action.

mealLogging.title: Meal logging
mealLogging.description: Log meals and servings with a nutrition-first workflow.

chatCoach.title: Coach chat
chatCoach.description: Ask Loggy about macros, training, or meal ideas.

chatCoach2.title: Coach workout advice
chatCoach2.description: Get tailored exercise suggestions and recovery tips from your AI coach.

bodyMetrics.title: Body metrics
bodyMetrics.description: Review weight trends and body composition insights.

workouts.title: Workout library
workouts.description: Browse and launch your training plans from one place.

createWorkout.title: Create workout
createWorkout.description: Start a routine from scratch or with AI.

today.title: Today's nutrition
today.description: See calories and macros for the day at a glance.

settings.title: Local data settings
settings.description: Manage exports, imports, and local data controls.

features.title: Built for Performance & Privacy
features.description: No telemetry, no tracking, no subscription. Just a powerful engine for your fitness journey.

strengthTracking.title: Offline Strength Tracking
strengthTracking.description: Log your sets and reps directly to your device. No internet required. Your data never leaves your pocket.

privacyFirst.title: Privacy First
privacyFirst.description: No sign-ups, no logins, and no third-party tracking. Musclog respects your digital sovereignty.

openSource.title: Open Source
openSource.description: Musclog is community-driven. Audit the code, contribute features, or fork it to make it your own.

featureGrid.title: Everything You Need to Train Smarter
featureGrid.description: From AI-powered coaching to detailed analytics, Musclog gives you the tools to take full control of your fitness journey.

aiCoach.title: AI Coach (Loggy)
aiCoach.description: Chat with your AI fitness coach for personalized workout plans, nutrition advice, and meal photo estimation.

barcode.title: Barcode Scanner
barcode.description: Quickly add products by scanning barcodes. Access the Open Food Facts database with millions of products.

checkins.title: Weekly Check-ins
checkins.description: Automated 7-day analysis of your progress with dynamic goal adjustments to keep you on track.

analytics.title: Progress Analytics
analytics.description: Interactive charts for weight, body fat, and lifting progress. Track your TDEE with real data.

cycle.title: Cycle Awareness
cycle.description: Track menstrual phases and receive training recommendations optimized for your cycle.

templates.title: Workout Templates
templates.description: Create custom routines like PPL or Upper/Lower. Access an extensive exercise library with muscle targeting.

healthConnect.title: Health Connect
healthConnect.description: Sync with Google Health Connect to share data with other health and fitness apps on Android.

encryption.title: Encrypted Storage
encryption.description: Your sensitive health data is encrypted at rest using AES. Export anytime as JSON or Excel.

howItWorks.title: How It Works
howItWorks.description: Get started in minutes. No complicated setup, no subscriptions, no data harvesting.

step1.title: Download & Go
step1.description: Install Musclog from the App Store or Google Play. No account required — just open and start tracking.

step2.title: Log Your Training
step2.description: Record sets, reps, and weights. Scan barcodes or snap photos to log meals. Everything stays on your device.

step3.title: Track Progress
step3.description: Review weekly check-ins, visualize trends, and let the AI coach help you optimize your approach.

testimonial.quote: "Musclog is the first app that respects my privacy while providing a world-class training experience. Local-first is the future."
testimonial.author: Laura F.

cta.title: Ready to start?
cta.description: Join thousands of athletes who use Musclog to track their progress without compromising privacy.

gameboy.title: Musclog on a Game Boy
gameboy.subtitle: We built a tiny Game Boy Color version of Musclog. Press play and try it right here in your browser.

gameboy.controlsHint: Desktop: arrow keys or WASD to move, X = A, Z = B, Enter = Start, Shift = Select. On touch devices, use the on-screen buttons.

hero.title: Import the exported data from your Musclog app here
hero.description: Drop in your Musclog JSON export to unlock a browser-based progress dashboard with weight trends, nutrition patterns, workout volume, and key correlations rendered for larger screens.

weight.title: Weight and body composition
weight.description: Review scale trends, body-fat changes, FFMI, and body measurements in one place.

nutrition.title: Nutrition and TDEE
nutrition.description: Inspect calorie intake, macro balance, and average intake snapshots from your imported log.

training.title: Training and correlations
training.description: Compare workout volume, mood, and recovery-style signals on a wider desktop canvas.

importCard.title: Bring your app export into this browser
importCard.description: Choose the JSON export created from the Musclog mobile app. The import runs locally in your browser storage and never leaves your device.
importCard.passphrasePlaceholder: Optional, only if your export was encrypted
importCard.genericError: Could not import this file. Please confirm it is a valid Musclog export.
importCard.localOnlyDescription: This page reads your export into the local web database only, so you can explore progress data without uploading it to a server.

dashboard.title: Your Musclog progress, redesigned for the desktop web
dashboard.description: This dashboard uses the same local database and progress calculations as the app, but presents them in a roomier desktop layout tuned for scanning trends and comparing patterns.
dashboard.localStorageHint: Data is stored in your browser's local Musclog database.

stats.weightHint: Latest weight in your selected range.
stats.caloriesHint: Average calories across {{count}} logged nutrition days.
stats.workoutsHint: Completed workout entries in the current range.
stats.tdeeHint: Current estimated maintenance based on available data.

bodyComposition.title: Body composition overview
bodyComposition.description: Your primary body metrics are surfaced first so the biggest shifts are visible at a glance.

nutrition.title: Nutrition trends
nutrition.description: Daily calorie totals and recent macro balance make it easier to connect intake with the outcomes you are seeing.

training.title: Training output
training.description: Workout volume and sets per muscle group show where your training effort has actually been going.

correlations.title: Correlation views
correlations.description: These desktop scatter views make it easier to inspect whether intake and body-composition changes move together.

mood.title: Mood snapshots
mood.description: Mood entries stay visible as a lightweight readiness layer beside the bigger body and training trends.

reminders.title: Reminder adherence
reminders.description: Water and supplement reminders now show up as completion trends, so you can review consistency alongside your other recovery signals.

measurements.title: Measurements
measurements.description: When circumference logs exist, they are surfaced here as compact trend cards.
measurements.empty: No body measurements were found in the imported range.

dataProtection.title: Data Protection
dataProtection.description: Comprehensive technical and organizational measures safeguard your data.

localFirst.title: Local-First Storage
localFirst.description: Your data is stored primarily on your device, not our servers.

security.title: Security First
security.description: Advanced encryption and access controls protect your information.

privacy.intro: This Privacy Policy describes Our policies and procedures on the collection, use, storage and disclosure of Your information when You use Musclog. It applies both to the Musclog application and to the Musclog website, although the categories of data processed in each context are different. This Privacy Policy also explains Your privacy rights and how the law protects You.

scope.title: Scope of This Privacy Policy
scope.content: Musclog is made available through both an application and a public website. Because those two services operate differently, the applicable privacy practices are summarized separately below and described in more detail throughout this Privacy Policy.

interpretation.title: Interpretation and Definitions
interpretation.content: The words of which the initial letter is capitalized have meanings defined under the following conditions. The following definitions shall have the same meaning regardless of whether they appear in singular or in plural.

account.def: means a unique account created for You to access our Service or parts of our Service.

application.def: refers to Musclog, the software program provided by Musclog.

company.def: (referred to as either "Musclog", "We", "Us" or "Our" in this Agreement) refers to Musclog.

device.def: means any device that can access the Service such as a computer, a cellphone or a digital tablet.

personalData.def: While using Our Service, We may ask You to provide Us with certain personally identifiable information that can be used to contact or identify You.

service.def: refers to the Musclog application and the Musclog website, unless stated otherwise in the relevant section.

serviceProvider.def: means any natural or legal person who processes the data on behalf of Musclog.

usageData.def: Usage Data is collected automatically when using the Service, including device information, IP address, browser type, and diagnostic data.

you.def: means the individual accessing or using the Service, or Musclog, or other legal entity on behalf of which such individual is accessing or using the Service, as applicable.

collecting.title: Collecting and Using Your Personal Data
collecting.content: While using Our Service, We may ask You to provide Us with certain personally identifiable information that can be used to contact or identify You.

typesCollected.title: Types of Data Collected
typesCollected.content: While using Our Service, We may ask You to provide Us with certain personally identifiable information that can be used to contact or identify You. Personally identifiable information may include, but is not limited to:
items: First name and last name
Usage Data

googleOAuth.title: Google OAuth Data
googleOAuth.content: Our application integrates Google OAuth for the following purposes:
items: To obtain your name to personalize your user experience.
To access Google Generative AI capabilities to enhance application features.
All data is stored offline on your device and not uploaded to our servers.
We do not share your data with third parties for marketing or other purposes.
Google OAuth is optional and can be disabled in the application settings.

usageData.title: Usage Data
usageData.content: Usage Data is collected automatically when using the Service. Usage Data may include information such as Your Device's Internet Protocol address (e.g. IP address), browser type, browser version, the pages of our Service that You visit, the time and date of Your visit, the time spent on those pages, unique device identifiers and other diagnostic data. When You access the Service by or through a mobile device, We may collect certain information automatically, including, but not limited to, the type of mobile device You use, Your mobile device unique ID, the IP address of Your mobile device, Your mobile operating system, the type of mobile Internet browser You use, unique device identifiers and other diagnostic data. We may also collect information that Your browser sends whenever You visit our Service or when You access the Service by or through a mobile device.

appPermissions.title: Information Collected while Using the Application
appPermissions.content: While using Our Application, in order to provide features of Our Application, We may collect, with Your prior permission:
items: Activity recognition data to track your workouts and fitness activities.
Body sensor data to monitor heart rate, steps, and other health metrics.
Internet access to connect to our servers and provide online functionalities.
Calendar data to integrate with your schedule and plan your workouts.
External storage access to read and write workout data and other information.
Permission to receive boot completed notifications to restart any scheduled tasks after your device reboots.
System alert window permission to display important notifications over other apps.
Vibrate permission to provide haptic feedback during workouts and notifications.
Wake lock permission to keep the app running during workouts and prevent the device from sleeping.
Health data such as active calories burned, body fat, bone mass, heart rate, height, lean body mass, nutrition, steps, total calories burned, and weight to provide comprehensive fitness tracking.

optionalAi.title: Optional AI Features and Third-Party LLM Providers
optionalAi.content1: The Musclog application can be used fully offline, except for optional requests to external nutrition data sources. By default, Musclog does not send your workout, nutrition, health, or profile data to our servers.
optionalAi.content2: Musclog also offers optional AI features that require you to configure your own provider, such as OpenAI, Google Gemini, Apple Intelligence on iOS, or a local/self-hosted LLM endpoint. These features are disabled unless you explicitly enable them.
optionalAi.content3: If you choose to use an AI feature, the data that you decide to submit will be sent to the provider or endpoint you configure. If you use a self-hosted local model under your own control, that data may remain within your own environment. If you use a third-party hosted endpoint, including a hosted local-model service, that provider may process the data in accordance with its own terms and privacy practices. Musclog does not control how external providers process data you send to them.
optionalAi.content4: Musclog may also offer a Musclog free-tier LLM option. When you use that option, your request is routed through Cloudflare AI Gateway and then forwarded using a Musclog-managed free-tier OpenAI or Google Gemini API key. If you choose to use this feature, you should understand that your request may be processed through Cloudflare and by the downstream model provider selected by Musclog for that request.
optionalAi.content5: At present, Musclog supports OpenAI, Google Gemini, Apple Intelligence on iOS, compatible local or self-hosted LLM endpoints, and the Musclog free-tier LLM route. You should review the applicable privacy policies before enabling third-party providers or accepting the free-tier option, including:

trainingData.title: Voluntary Training Data Contribution
trainingData.content1: If you own a WT9011DCL BLE sensor device, connect it to the app, and use it to track your reps, Musclog will generate local sensor recording files on your device. These files are only created when this specific device is actively used for rep tracking. They are stored exclusively on your device and are never uploaded, synced, or transmitted to any server automatically — there is no opt-in or opt-out toggle, and no automatic data sharing of any kind.
trainingData.content2: If you would like to contribute your data to help improve Musclog's fitness tracking models, you may share these files manually — for example, by email or through a cloud storage service such as Google Drive. There is no in-app mechanism for this; any sharing is entirely at your own initiative and discretion.
trainingData.content3: Data you voluntarily share in this way may be used to train, test, and improve machine learning models. The resulting model may be released publicly as open-source software. By choosing to share your data, you acknowledge that it may become part of a publicly accessible dataset, and you grant permission for it to be copied, stored, processed, and distributed as part of the model development process. You should only share data that you have the right to share and that you are comfortable making publicly available.

useOfData.title: Use of Your Personal Data
items: To provide and maintain our Service, including to monitor the usage of our Service.
To provide optional AI-powered features only when you explicitly enable them and configure your own provider or endpoint.
To manage Your Account: to manage Your registration as a user of the Service.
For the performance of a contract: the development, compliance and undertaking of the purchase contract for the products, items or services You have purchased.
To contact You: To contact You by email, telephone calls, SMS, or other equivalent forms of electronic communication.
To provide You with news, special offers and general information about other goods, services and events.
To manage Your requests: To attend and manage Your requests to Us.
For business transfers: We may use Your information to evaluate or conduct a merger, divestiture, restructuring, reorganization, dissolution, or other sale or transfer of some or all of Our assets.

For other purposes: We may use Your information for other purposes, such as data analysis, identifying usage trends, determining the effectiveness of our promotional campaigns and to evaluate and improve our Service.

sharing.title: Sharing Your Personal Data
items: With Service Providers: We may share Your personal information with Service Providers to monitor and analyze the use of our Service, to contact You.
For business transfers: We may share or transfer Your personal information in connection with, or during negotiations of, any merger, sale of Company assets, financing, or acquisition.
With Affiliates: We may share Your information with Our affiliates, in which case we will require those affiliates to honor this Privacy Policy.
With business partners: We may share Your information with Our business partners to offer You certain products, services or promotions.
With other users: when You share personal information or otherwise interact in the public areas with other users, such information may be viewed by all users.
With Your consent: We may disclose Your personal information for any other purpose with Your consent.

retention.title: Retention of Your Personal Data
retention.content: Musclog will retain Your Personal Data only for as long as is necessary for the purposes set out in this Privacy Policy. We will retain and use Your Personal Data to the extent necessary to comply with our legal obligations, resolve disputes, and enforce our legal agreements and policies. Musclog will also retain Usage Data for internal analysis purposes. Usage Data is generally retained for a shorter period of time, except when this data is used to strengthen the security or to improve the functionality of Our Service, or We are legally obligated to retain this data for longer time periods.

transfer.title: Transfer of Your Personal Data
transfer.content: Your information, including Personal Data, is processed at Musclog's operating offices and in any other places where the parties involved in the processing are located. It means that this information may be transferred to — and maintained on — computers located outside of Your state, province, country or other governmental jurisdiction where the data protection laws may differ than those from Your jurisdiction. Your consent to this Privacy Policy followed by Your submission of such information represents Your agreement to that transfer. Musclog will take all steps reasonably necessary to ensure that Your data is treated securely and in accordance with this Privacy Policy.

deletion.title: Delete Your Personal Data
deletion.content: You have the right to delete or request that We assist in deleting the Personal Data that We have collected about You. Our Service may give You the ability to delete certain information about You from within the Service. You may update, amend, or delete Your information at any time by signing in to Your Account, if you have one, and visiting the account settings section that allows you to manage Your personal information. You may also contact Us to request access to, correct, or delete any personal information that You have provided to Us. Please note, however, that We may need to retain certain information when we have a legal obligation or lawful basis to do so.

disclosure.title: Disclosure of Your Personal Data
disclosure.content: Musclog may disclose Your Personal Data in the good faith belief that such action is necessary to:
items: Comply with a legal obligation
Protect and defend the rights or property of Musclog
Prevent or investigate possible wrongdoing in connection with the Service
Protect the personal safety of Users of the Service or the public
Protect against legal liability

security.title: Security of Your Personal Data
security.content: The security of Your Personal Data is important to Us, but remember that no method of transmission over the Internet, or method of electronic storage is 100% secure. While We strive to use commercially acceptable means to protect Your Personal Data, We cannot guarantee its absolute security. We employ comprehensive technical and organizational measures to safeguard your personal data from unauthorized or unlawful processing, as well as accidental loss, destruction, or damage. These measures include advanced encryption protocols for data in transit and at rest, stringent access controls, and regular security assessments.

childrenPrivacy.title: Children's Privacy
childrenPrivacy.content: Our Service does not address anyone under the age of 13. We do not knowingly collect personally identifiable information from anyone under the age of 13. If You are a parent or guardian and You are aware that Your child has provided Us with Personal Data, please contact Us. If We become aware that We have collected Personal Data from anyone under the age of 13 without verification of parental consent, We take steps to remove that information from Our servers. If We need to rely on consent as a legal basis for processing Your information and Your country requires consent from a parent, We may require Your parent's consent before We collect and use that information.

links.title: Links to Other Websites
links.content: Our Service may contain links to other websites that are not operated by Us. If You click on a third party link, You will be directed to that third party's site. We strongly advise You to review the Privacy Policy of every site You visit. We have no control over and assume no responsibility for the content, privacy policies or practices of any third party sites or services.

changes.title: Changes to this Privacy Policy
changes.content: We may update Our Privacy Policy from time to time. We will notify You of any changes by posting the new Privacy Policy on this page. We will let You know via email and/or a prominent notice on Our Service, prior to the change becoming effective and update the "Last updated" date at the top of this Privacy Policy. You are advised to review this Privacy Policy periodically for any changes. Changes to this Privacy Policy are effective when they are posted on this page.

googlePlay.title: Google Play Store Data Safety Labels
googlePlay.content: Our data safety labels on the Google Play Store are meticulously aligned with the disclosures provided in this privacy policy. We are committed to maintaining a high standard of transparency and ensuring full compliance with all Google Play Store requirements and guidelines. The data collected is strictly utilized to provide or enhance the functionality and features of your application. This data usage is confined to improving user experience and application performance, and under no circumstances is it used for any purposes outside this scope, such as marketing or selling to third parties. Access to any user data is strictly regulated and can only occur after obtaining explicit consent from the user.

errorReportingFallback.title: Anonymous Crash Reporting — Database Failure Exception
errorReportingFallback.content: Musclog lets you opt in or out of anonymous bug reports through the app settings. That preference is stored locally in your device's database. In the rare event of a critical database failure — for example, when a database migration fails to complete — the app cannot access the local database to read your preference. In this specific circumstance only, Musclog will send one anonymous crash report to our error-tracking service (Sentry) to help us identify and fix the issue. The report contains no personal data: only the type of error, the database schema version numbers (before and after the failed migration), your device model, and your app version. This exception exists solely to surface critical failures that would otherwise be invisible to us, and does not affect normal operation.

analytics.title: Website Analytics
analytics.content1: This website uses Vercel Web Analytics, a cookieless and privacy-first analytics service. It collects no personal identifiers and uses no cookies — visitors are identified only by an anonymous hash that resets every 24 hours. No consent is required for this tool under the ePrivacy Directive.
analytics.content2: This website also uses Google Analytics 4 (GA4), a service provided by Google LLC, to collect anonymized data about how visitors interact with the site. Analytics cookies are only set after you explicitly consent by clicking "Accept" in the cookie banner.
analytics.content3: When you consent to GA4, Google Analytics may collect:
items: Pages visited and time spent on each page
General geographic location (country or city level, not precise location)
Browser type, device type, and operating system
Referring website or search engine
Anonymous interaction events (e.g. link clicks)
analytics.content4: Legal basis for GA4: Consent (GDPR Art. 6(1)(a)). You may withdraw your consent at any time by clicking "Cookie settings" in the page footer. Withdrawal of consent will prevent any further collection and does not affect the lawfulness of processing carried out before withdrawal.
analytics.content5: GA4 data is retained in Google Analytics for 14 months by default. Google acts as a data processor under a Data Processing Agreement. For more information on how Google handles data, see the
analytics.content6: Your consent choice is stored locally in your browser (localStorage) and is never sent to our servers. It is used solely to remember your preference between visits.

app.title: Within the Musclog Application
app.summary: The Musclog application is designed primarily as a local-first product. Most user data remains stored on your device and is not transmitted to our servers.
items: All data you enter into the application is stored locally on your device.
The application does not require an account and does not provide cloud synchronization.
We do not have access to your health or fitness data stored within the application.
Sensitive data stored by the application is encrypted at rest using AES.

website.title: On This Website
website.summary: The website processes a narrower set of data, primarily relating to website operation, language preference, analytics, and cookie consent management.
items: This website uses Vercel Analytics to measure anonymous, cookieless traffic data without requiring consent.
This website uses Google Analytics 4 only after you provide explicit consent through the cookie banner.
Your language preference is stored locally in your browser and is not transmitted to our servers.
Your cookie consent choice is stored locally in your browser and is not transmitted to our servers.

contact.title: Contact Us
contact.content: If you have any questions about this Privacy Policy, You can contact us by email:

agreement.title: User Agreement
agreement.description: By using this app, you agree to these terms.

liability.title: Limited Liability
liability.description: We are not liable for health decisions made using this app.

warranty.title: No Warranty
warranty.description: The app is provided as-is without any warranties.

terms.intro: Please read these Terms and Conditions carefully before using the Musclog application. By accessing or using our service, you agree to be bound by these terms.

interpretation.title: Interpretation & Definitions
interpretation.content1: The words of which the initial letter is capitalized have meanings defined under the following conditions. The following definitions shall have the same meaning regardless of whether they appear in singular or in plural.
interpretation.content2: For the purposes of these Terms and Conditions:

application.def: means the software program provided by Musclog, downloaded by you on any electronic device, named Musclog.

company.def: refers to Musclog, the developer and maintainer of this open-source application.

device.def: means any device that can access the Service such as a smartphone or tablet.

you.def: means the individual accessing or using the Service.

acknowledgment.title: Acknowledgment
acknowledgment.content1: These are the Terms and Conditions governing the use of this Service and the agreement that operates between you and the Company. By accessing or using the Service you agree to be bound by these Terms and Conditions.
acknowledgment.content2: Your access to and use of the Service is also conditioned on your acceptance of and compliance with the Privacy Policy of the Company.
acknowledgment.content3: Musclog is a local-first, open-source fitness tracker. No account is required. Your data stays on your device and is never sent to our servers.

links.title: Links to Other Websites
links.content: Our Service may contain links to third-party websites or services that are not owned or controlled by the Company. We have no control over and assume no responsibility for the content, privacy policies, or practices of any third-party websites or services.

termination.title: Termination
termination.content: We may terminate or suspend your access immediately, without prior notice or liability, for any reason whatsoever, including without limitation if you breach these Terms and Conditions. Upon termination, your right to use the Service will cease immediately.

liability.title: Limitation of Liability
liability.content1: To the maximum extent permitted by applicable law, in no event shall the Company be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential or punitive damages, including without limitation, loss of profits, data, use, goodwill, or other intangible losses.
liability.content2: This application is not a medical device. Always consult a qualified healthcare professional before making health or fitness decisions based on data from this app.

disclaimer.title: Disclaimer
disclaimer.content1: The Service is provided to you "AS IS" and "AS AVAILABLE" and with all faults and defects without warranty of any kind.
disclaimer.content2: Without limiting the foregoing, the Company provides no warranty or undertaking, and makes no representation of any kind that the Service will meet your requirements, achieve any intended results, or be compatible or work with any other software, applications, systems, or services.

governingLaw.title: Governing Law
governingLaw.content1: The laws of the country excluding its conflicts of law rules shall govern this Terms and your use of the Service.
governingLaw.content2: Your local consumer protection laws may provide you with additional rights. Nothing in these Terms affects any rights you may have as a consumer under applicable law.

changes.title: Changes to These Terms
changes.content: We reserve the right, at our sole discretion, to modify or replace these Terms at any time. We will try to provide at least 30 days' notice prior to any new terms taking effect. By continuing to access or use our Service after those revisions become effective, you agree to be bound by the revised terms.

contact.description: Have a question, found a bug, or want to contribute? We'd love to hear from you.

privacy.title: Privacy First
privacy.description: We don't store anything you send here. Your message goes directly to our email — no servers, no logs.

calculator.subtitle: Precision-engineered data for the high-performance athlete. Calculate your biological baseline and optimize your trajectory.

form.title: FITNESS PROFILE
form.genderNote: These formulas come from older research that only recognized two groups, so they can't reflect everyone. Pick whichever best matches your current body and hormones (e.g. if you're on hormone therapy), or choose "Other" for averaged values.

results.bmrDesc: Basal Metabolic Rate: energy expended at rest.
results.tdeeDesc: Total Daily Energy Expenditure based on activity.
results.bmiDesc: Body Mass Index: normalized height to weight ratio.
results.ffmiDesc: Fat-Free Mass Index: muscle mass indicator.

exercises.pageDescription: Browse our full collection of exercises — filter by muscle group, mechanic, or search by name.
exercises.customNote: These are the exercises bundled with the app. You can also create your own custom exercises directly inside Musclog.

faq.description: Every number Musclog shows you comes from peer-reviewed research. This page explains the models, formulas, and studies behind each calculation — with links to the original sources.

footer.text: Found an inaccuracy or a newer study that supersedes one of these sources?
footer.suffix: — the science is always evolving and so is Musclog.

items.question: What is Musclog?
items.answer: Musclog is a privacy-first fitness app for tracking workouts, nutrition, and body composition. All your data is stored locally on your device — nothing is sent to any server unless you explicitly enable optional AI features.

items.question: Does the app work without an internet connection?
items.answer: Yes. All core features — logging workouts, tracking meals, viewing progress, and running the nutrition calculator — work fully offline. An internet connection is only needed for optional AI-powered features (meal recognition, AI coach), food database lookups from external sources, and Health Connect / HealthKit sync.

items.question: How accurate are the nutrition calculations?
items.answer: The calculator uses well-validated population equations (Katch-McArdle / Mifflin-St Jeor for BMR, Hall 2008 body-composition model for projections). No equation is perfectly accurate for every individual — typical error for BMR equations is ±10 %. The best results come from feeding the app real tracking data over 2–4 weeks so it can compute your empirical TDEE directly from calories in vs. weight change.

items.question: What units does the app support?
items.answer: The database always stores metric values (kg, cm, g, kcal). You can switch to imperial (lbs, inches, oz) in Settings at any time — the app converts all displayed values automatically. Body fat percentage, BMI, and FFMI are unitless and are never converted.

items.question: How is my Basal Metabolic Rate (BMR) calculated?
items.answer: Musclog uses two equations depending on whether you have provided a body fat percentage.
bullets: Katch-McArdle (preferred when BF % is available): BMR = 370 + 21.6 × Lean Body Mass (kg). Because it ignores fat mass — which has very low metabolic activity — this equation is more accurate for athletes and people with non-average body compositions.

Mifflin-St Jeor (default, no BF % needed): BMR = 10 × weight + 6.25 × height − 5 × age ± gender constant (+5 male, −161 female). Validated as the most accurate general-population BMR equation across several meta-analyses.

citations.label: Katch-McArdle (1996)
Mifflin et al. (1990)
Frankenfield et al. meta-analysis (2005)

items.question: How is my Total Daily Energy Expenditure (TDEE) estimated?
items.answer: Musclog uses two modes, automatically choosing the best one for your data.
bullets: Statistical (new users): TDEE = BMR × Activity Multiplier. Multipliers range from 1.2 (sedentary) to 1.9 (very active + physical job), based on the Harris-Benedict / Mifflin-St Jeor scale.

Empirical / Observed TDEE (preferred, requires ≥2 weeks of food + weight data): Applies the First Law of Thermodynamics directly. TDEE = (Calories in − Energy stored in tissue) / Days. Fat tissue stores 7,730 kcal/kg when catabolised and costs 8,840 kcal/kg to build; muscle stores 1,250 kcal/kg and costs 3,900 kcal/kg. These asymmetric values account for thermodynamic synthesis efficiency.

TDEE Drift Correction: The empirical TDEE represents the mid-period average. To project it to the current body state, three stacked adjustments are applied: (1) Resting Tissue Drop — lean tissue burns ~13 kcal/kg/day and fat ~4.5 kcal/kg/day (Elia 1992); (2) Activity-Scaled Drop — mechanical cost of movement scales with body mass; (3) Adaptive Thermogenesis penalty of 20 kcal/day per kg of body mass lost, reflecting NEAT suppression and hormonal adaptation (leptin, thyroid).

citations.label: Hall (2008) — Energy deficit per unit weight loss
Elia (1992) — RMR tissue coefficients
Müller et al. (2022) — Adaptive thermogenesis
Hall et al. (NIH) — Dynamic energy balance

items.question: How does providing body fat percentage improve the calculation?
items.answer: When a valid body fat percentage (5–60 %) is entered, three things change:
bullets: BMR switches to Katch-McArdle, which is body-composition-aware and ignores metabolically inert fat mass.

The fat/lean split of weight changes is computed from the Forbes Curve (Hall 2007), solved analytically via the Lambert W function. The key insight: leaner individuals lose a higher fraction of lean mass per kg because the body defends fat stores more aggressively. A person at 12 % BF loses tissue at roughly 5,800–6,200 kcal/kg, far below the classical ‘7,700 kcal/kg’ rule which assumes pure fat loss.
A ±4 % uncertainty band (typical BIA/skinfold error) generates a realistic min/max calorie range instead of a single number, communicating the inherent imprecision of body fat measurement.
items.formulaCaption: Hall (2008) blended energy density — fat fraction determined by Forbes curve / Lambert W

citations.label: Forbes (1987) — Body Composition: Growth, Aging, Nutrition, and Activity
Hall (2007) — Predicting metabolic adaptation, body weight, and body fat changes
Hall (2008) — Required Energy Deficit per unit Weight Loss

items.question: How is my recommended calorie deficit or surplus set?
items.answer: Musclog scales the calorie adjustment to your body weight, rather than using a fixed ±500 kcal, and clamps it to evidence-based safe ranges.
bullets: Cut (weight loss): The recommended weekly loss rate is 0.5 % of body weight per week by default. When body fat is known, this is refined using ACE clinical thresholds — lean individuals (BF < 10 % male / < 20 % female) get a conservative 0.35 %/week to protect muscle, while higher-BF individuals can safely lose at up to 0.65 %/week. The resulting daily deficit is clamped to 250–750 kcal to prevent severe metabolic adaptation.

Bulk (weight gain): A surplus of approximately 0.25 % body weight per week is targeted, which minimises fat spillover while supporting muscle protein synthesis. The surplus is clamped to 150–400 kcal/day.
Minimum calorie floors: 1,200 kcal/day for females, 1,500 kcal/day for males, and never below 80 % of BMR — consistent with IOM / National Academies guidelines.

citations.label: Garthe et al. (2011) — Rate of weight loss and body composition
Iraki et al. (2019) — Nutrition recommendations for natural bodybuilders
ACE — Body fat percentage classifications

items.question: How does the weight projection model work?
items.answer: The projection uses two phases to accurately predict 90-day weight change.
bullets: Early phase — Glycogen depletion: The whole-body glycogen pool is approximately 15 g/kg of body weight (dry), bound to about 3 g of water each, totalling roughly 6 % of body weight as hydrated glycogen. Only the portion of the cumulative calorie deficit that fits within this glycogen energy pool (~4,206 kcal/kg dry) drives the early “water weight” loss. This prevents wildly over-predicting early losses for aggressive deficits.

Steady-state phase: All remaining deficit drives fat/lean tissue loss at the composition-aware energy density from the Hall/Forbes model. The reported “weekly rate” always reflects this steady-state figure — what users experience after the first few days.

Surplus projection: Glycogen stores are assumed to be full, so all surplus goes to fat/lean synthesis at experience-weighted costs (beginner ~5,300 kcal/kg gained; intermediate ~6,370 kcal/kg; advanced ~7,430 kcal/kg).

citations.label: Hall (2008) — Energy density of weight loss tissue
Schoenfeld et al. — Hypertrophy effect sizes, trained vs untrained

items.question: How are my daily macros calculated?
items.answer: Macro splits are based on fitness goal, using IOM AMDR ranges as the framework and ISSN/ACSM sport-specific research for refinement.

head: Goal
Carbs
Protein
Fat
Rationale
rows: High protein to preserve muscle in deficit (ISSN/Helms 2014)
Protein for MPS; carbs support volume training
Carbs for glycolytic demand of heavy compound work
Centered within IOM AMDR for general fitness

items.answer2: For Weight Loss and Hypertrophy goals, when body fat % is known, protein is upgraded from a percentage target to an absolute FFM-based target (2.7 g/kg FFM for weight loss; 2.0 g/kg FFM for hypertrophy), with remaining calories split proportionally between carbs and fats.

citations.label: IOM Dietary Reference Intakes (2005) — AMDR
Jäger et al. (2017) — ISSN Protein and Exercise Position Stand
Helms et al. (2014) — Evidence-based bodybuilding nutrition recommendations
Thomas et al. (2016) — ACSM/ADA/DC Joint Position Stand

items.question: How does lifting experience affect the bulk calculation?
items.answer: The fraction of gained weight that is lean mass vs fat depends heavily on training history. Untrained individuals have far greater hypertrophy potential than advanced lifters who have already captured most of their genetic muscle ceiling.
bullets: Beginner: ~60 % lean / 40 % fat per kg gained. Effective build cost ≈ 5,304 kcal/kg.
Intermediate: ~50 % lean / 50 % fat. Effective build cost ≈ 6,370 kcal/kg.
Advanced: ~40 % lean / 60 % fat. Effective build cost ≈ 7,434 kcal/kg.

These splits use the thermodynamic build costs: 8,840 kcal/kg for fat and 3,900 kcal/kg for muscle, reflecting ~87 % synthesis efficiency for fat and ~32 % efficiency for muscle (most muscle synthesis energy is lost as heat).

citations.label: Schoenfeld et al. — Resistance training load meta-analysis
Sports Med (2023) — Plateau in muscle growth with resistance training
NCBI (2022) — Thermodynamic efficiency of muscle protein synthesis

items.question: How is RMR adjusted for obesity or older age?
items.answer: Two evidence-based penalty multipliers are applied to the Resting Metabolic Rate tissue coefficients (lean: 13 kcal/kg/day; fat: 4.5 kcal/kg/day):
bullets: BMI > 30 (Obesity): −2 % reduction to both lean and fat tissue coefficients, reflecting impaired mitochondrial function in obese individuals (Müller et al. 2013).
Age > 50: −3 % reduction, reflecting the decline in cellular turnover rate (St-Onge & Gallagher 2010).
Both penalties stack additively when applicable (e.g. obese person over 50 → −5 % total).

citations.label: Müller et al. (2013) — Metabolic adaptation to caloric restriction
St-Onge & Gallagher (2010) — Body composition changes with aging

items.question: How are one-rep max (1RM) estimates calculated?
items.answer: Musclog calculates 1RM using an ensemble of seven published formulas and averages their outputs. Averaging reduces the systematic error of any single formula, which each have different accuracy profiles across rep ranges.
items.answer2: Reps in Reserve (RIR) is added to actual reps before applying any formula (adjusted_reps = reps + RIR). This maps submaximal effort to a full-effort equivalent, so a set of 8 reps with 2 RIR is treated as 10 reps at failure for 1RM estimation purposes.

citations.label: Brzycki (1993)
Epley (1985)
Lander (1985)
Lombardi (1989)
Mayhew et al. (1992)
O'Connor et al. (1989)
Wathen (1994)

items.question: How is workout volume calculated?
items.answer: Musclog uses the average 1RM estimate as the unit of volume for each set, rather than raw tonnage (weight × reps). This produces a more meaningful measure because the 1RM normalises for the fact that higher-rep sets represent more total effort than the raw weight would suggest.
bullets: For bodyweight exercises, the user's current body weight is added to the bar weight before the 1RM calculation.
Total session volume = sum of average 1RM estimates across all sets in the session.
Progressive overload tracking compares this number across sessions for the same exercises.

items.question: How are calories burned from daily steps calculated?
items.answer: Musclog uses the ACSM metabolic walking equations to compute net (not gross) calorie expenditure from step count. Using net expenditure prevents double-counting against the BMR/TDEE already factored in.
items.formulaCaption: Derived from ACSM VO₂ walking equation. Time cancels out: speed (m/min) × duration (min) = distance (m). 1 L O₂ ≈ 5 kcal, so ÷1000 × 5 = ÷200.
bullets: Stride length is estimated from height using gender-specific empirical ratios: 0.415 × height (cm) / 100 for males, 0.413 for females.
Distance = steps × stride length.
The derivation eliminates the need to know cadence, removing the “cadence paradox” where faster walking looks identical to slower walking at the same step count.

citations.label: ACSM Metabolic Equations — Walking VO₂
Pratama et al. (2012) — Stride length estimation
Meta-Analysis — Stride length and height ratios

items.question: How does the app calculate weight and reps for a target RIR?
items.answer: Two inverse 1RM problems are solved:
bullets: Weight for target RIR: All seven 1RM formulas are linear in weight (1RM = weight × f(reps, RIR)), so weight = 1RM / f(reps, RIR). The multiplier f is computed by calling the ensemble with weight = 1.

Reps for target weight: The averaged ensemble has no closed-form inverse, so the app uses binary search. Because 1RM estimates are monotonically increasing in reps, binary search converges within ~6 iterations for any practical rep range (1–50). See Helms et al. (2016) for RIR-based autoregulation research basis.

citations.label: Helms et al. (2016) — RPE and RIR-based autoregulation

items.question: Why does the app show a calorie range instead of one number?
items.answer: Body fat percentage measurements — whether from bioelectrical impedance (BIA), skinfold calipers, or DEXA — carry a typical measurement error of ±3–5 %. Musclog applies a ±4 % band (a conservative midpoint) to your entered body fat percentage and recalculates BMR, TDEE, and target calories at both extremes.
bullets: Min target calories = calculation at BF% + 4 % (more fat → less lean mass → lower BMR).
Max target calories = calculation at BF% − 4 % (less fat → more lean mass → higher BMR).
The single “target” in the middle is the best-estimate result using your entered BF% directly.
This range communicates realistic uncertainty and helps you understand that the “correct” number for you personally may sit anywhere within the band.

items.question: What is the Forbes Curve and why does it matter?
items.answer: The Forbes Curve, developed by Gilbert Forbes (1987) and further formalised by Kevin Hall (2007), describes how the body partitions weight changes between fat mass and lean mass. It has a critical, counter-intuitive implication:
bullets: Leaner people sacrifice more lean mass per kg lost — the body defends fat stores more aggressively when fat reserves are low.
Conversely, people with high body fat lose a higher proportion of fat per kg, making their effective energy density closer to the classic 7,700 kcal/kg rule.

The curve is parameterised by a constant C (≈13.8 for males, ≈10.4 for females), solved using the Lambert W function (principal branch). Musclog implements this solver numerically with Newton’s method to avoid any library dependency.
Without this model, cuts for lean individuals are systematically under-estimated — the classic rule treats all weight loss as fat and would over-predict how much fat is actually lost.

citations.label: Forbes (1987) — Human Body Composition
Hall (2007) — PMC2376748 — Forbes curve formalisation

items.question: What is Adaptive Thermogenesis and how does the app account for it?
items.answer: Adaptive thermogenesis (AT) is the phenomenon where the body’s energy expenditure drops by more than can be explained by changes in body mass alone, in response to sustained caloric restriction. It is driven by:
bullets: Hormonal suppression: leptin falls with fat loss, reducing sympathetic nervous system activity and thyroid hormone output.
NEAT (Non-Exercise Activity Thermogenesis) reduction: the body unconsciously reduces fidgeting, posture changes, and spontaneous movement.
Mitochondrial efficiency changes: cells extract more ATP per calorie burned.

Musclog models AT as a 20 kcal/day penalty per kg of total body mass lost (using the midpoint of the Hall et al. range of 20–30 kcal/day/kg). This is applied in the TDEE drift correction when computing from empirical tracking data, and in the 90-day projection to show a realistic weight loss trajectory rather than a linear one.

citations.label: Müller et al. (2022) — Magnitude of AT in clinical trials
Hall et al. (NIH) — Dynamic energy balance model (20–30 kcal/kg range)

items.question: How is the "Calories Burned Today" estimate calculated?
items.answer: The real-time calorie burn estimate uses a circadian-weighted model rather than a simple linear division of your TDEE across 24 hours. Metabolic chamber and circadian biology research shows that energy expenditure is not uniform throughout the day — it follows a pronounced 24-hour rhythm driven by your internal biological clock, posture, meal timing (thermic effect of food, TEF), and spontaneous physical activity (NEAT).

head: Time Block
% of TDEE
Primary Driver
rows: Sleep onset, sympathetic withdrawal, SMR at 85–95 % of BMR
Circadian nadir (~5 AM), lowest core temperature and REE
Waking posture rise (+10 %), peak morning TEF
Lunch TEF, daytime NEAT and occupational movement
Circadian REE peak (~5 PM), core temperature peak
Evening wind-down, blunted dinner TEF, cooling core temperature

items.answer2: Each block is four hours (240 minutes). Since the 11 PM–3 AM block straddles midnight, the app splits it into two contiguous segments (00:00–03:00 and 23:00–24:00) so the calculation stays anchored to local midnight. Calories accumulated so far today = Σ (elapsed minutes in each completed or partial segment × block rate × TDEE). The current metabolic phase label (e.g. "Circadian Peak") reflects which block is active right now.

citations.label: Bo et al. (2015) — Meal timing & diet-induced thermogenesis — IJO
Manini (2010) — Energy expenditure and aging — Ageing Res Rev
Morris et al. (2015) — Circadian system & morning/evening TEF difference — Obesity
Poggiogalle et al. (2018) — Circadian regulation of energy metabolism — Metabolism
Ruddick-Collins et al. (2021) — Circadian RMR rhythms & thermic effect of food — JCEM
Sharma & Kavuru (2010) — Sleep and metabolism overview — Int J Endocrinol

items.question: How is the daily water intake recommendation calculated?
items.answer: Water recommendation scales linearly with TDEE at a ratio of 0.75 ml per kcal of daily energy expenditure. This is derived from two independently converging guidelines:
bullets: The U.S. Food and Nutrition Board (1945): “1 ml per kcal of energy expended” (Musclog uses 0.75 ml to represent the beverage fraction, as ~25 % of water comes from food).
Dietary surveys show that beverages contribute approximately 70–80 % of total water intake in adults (Gandy et al. 2016).

citations.label: PMC7692653 — Water intake from beverages vs food
PMC5946122 — Percentage of water intake from beverages

repMarker.description: Upload a workout video and sensor JSON to annotate rep boundaries
repMarker.dragDropVideo: Drag & drop video or click to browse
repMarker.dragDropJson: Drag & drop JSON or click to browse
repMarker.waitingEnd: Waiting for end — press E when rep ends
repMarker.noMarkers: No reps marked yet. Press S to mark the start of a rep.
repMarker.keyboardHint: S = mark start · E = mark end · Space = play/pause

repMarker.chartAccelerationSubtitle: raw accel · works for any lift
